paper.title_en: Spectroscopy of excited neutron configurations of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>54</mn></mmultiscripts></math>
paper.abstract_en: Despite its nucleon numbers not coinciding with the traditional nuclear magic numbers, the structure of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>54</mn></mmultiscripts></math> shows signatures of a doubly closed shell nucleus. This article details the structures of neutron-excited states up to beyond the two-neutron emission threshold populated by the <math><mrow><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>55</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>(</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>p</mi><mi>n</mi></mrow></math>) reaction at <math><mrow><mo>∼</mo><mn>200</mn></mrow></math> MeV/nucleon. Level energies and population cross sections were determined through <math><mi>γ</mi></math>-ray spectroscopy and invariant-mass spectroscopy of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>54</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. Large-scale shell model calculations with protons and neutrons allowed to occupy the <math><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>d</mi><mtext>-</mtext><mi>p</mi><mi>f</mi><mtext>-</mtext><mi>s</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>g</mi></mrow></math> orbitals were performed to predict energy levels and spectroscopic factors. Distorted wave impulse approximation calculations provided single-particle knockout cross sections and momentum distributions of the reaction products. The theoretical calculations reproduce the data well. Both theory and experiment are suggestive of a well-preserved shell structure at high excitation energy. Also, inelastic scattering of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>54</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, carried out within the same experiment, validates previously proposed level schemes.
